config.name: calib_baseline
config.hypothesis: stable baseline (decay_clamp 0.999, lr 2.5e-4); confirm tok_acc is non-zero and rising on the easier 4-key task — proves the proxy discriminates.

config.name: slowdecay099_steps600
config.hypothesis: slow_decay 0.97 lets the slow track decay to ~0 over 512 tokens (0.97^512~=2e-7), leaving only the clamped fast track; raising it to 0.99 (~1000x more slow-track retention) plus 600 steps gives real two-timescale retention and room for true retrieval to emerge past the ~0.30 format plateau.

config.name: lrmem4e4_dclamp995_steps500
config.hypothesis: the ~0.30 format plateau is an optimization-rate ceiling, not a retention one (exp002 showed more retention didn't help); push both LRs up ~60% (memory 2.5e-4->4e-4, coproduct 1.5e-4->2.5e-4) with a tighter decay_clamp 0.999->0.995 to absorb the extra energy and hold skip=0 — if tokacc rises past 0.30 the bottleneck is the optimizer, if it stays flat the plateau is fundamental (-> Phase 2).

config.name: rank32_steps600
config.hypothesis: the format plateau is a state-CAPACITY ceiling, not retention or optimization (both prior break attempts diverged with no gain): a rank-16 Kronecker-residual state is too low-rank to disentangle 4 key->value bindings, so doubling residual_rank to 32 at the proven-stable baseline (lr 2.5e-4, decay_clamp 0.999, slow_decay 0.97) + 600 steps lets true retrieval emerge past ~0.30.

config.name: coplr3e4_steps600
config.hypothesis: the format plateau means the coproduct key->value binding (W_q/W_k/W_v) is under-trained relative to the memory state; doubling lr_coproduct 1.5e-4->3e-4 while holding the stable lr_memory 2.5e-4 targets the retrieval mechanism directly — distinct from the three failed axes (retention-up, LR-up, capacity-up) and should lift tokacc past 0.30 if binding is the bottleneck.

config.name: lrmem15e4_steps800
config.hypothesis: the ~0.30 format plateau is a sharp local minimum that diverging upward pushes (LR/capacity/binding/retention) cannot escape; a LOWER lr_memory 2.5e-4->1.5e-4 with more steps 800 trades speed for finer, more stable optimization, giving the coproduct binding a longer runway to find true retrieval instead of settling for the format shortcut.

config.name: slowdecay090_steps500
config.hypothesis: exp006's surprise (divergence is slow state accumulation independent of LR) points at decay dynamics as the prime suspect; forgetting HARDER via slow_decay 0.97->0.90 bounds the slow-track state more aggressively, which should restore stability (kill the late divergence seen at slow_decay 0.97) AND test whether stale state interferes with retrieval — if a cleaner-forgetting state lifts tokacc past 0.30 it's both a beat and a mechanism diagnosis.

config.name: calib_baseline_seed3
config.hypothesis: all 7 prior runs use seed=0, so the 0.28-0.31 tokacc spread (incl. exp006's 'best' 0.308) may be eval/training noise not real config effects; re-running exp001's exact stable config with seed 3 measures true run-to-run variance — if it lands ~0.29+/-0.03 the plateau is a genuine seed-independent ceiling (-> Phase 2), if it shifts substantially seed-0 is a confound and earlier results need re-reading.

config.name: dclamp997_steps500
config.hypothesis: exp006 implicated slow state-accumulation as the divergence cause; decay_clamp is the fast-track forgetting floor that bounds retained state, but it was only ever tested confounded with high LR (exp003, 4e-4->diverged). Lowering decay_clamp 0.999->0.997 at the STABLE lr 2.5e-4 isolates whether the fast-track floor controls the late divergence independently, and tests whether a tighter state bound lets retrieval escape the format plateau the way slow_decay 0.90 (slow-track) did not.

config.name: nkeys8_steps500
config.hypothesis: the ~0.30 plateau at n_keys=4 may be partly a task-difficulty artifact — with only 4 keys the model can hit ~0.30 by digit-frequency guessing with no real retrieval pressure. Raising retrieval load to n_keys=8 tests whether the plateau holds under real retrieval demand: if tokacc collapses well below 0.30 the easy-task plateau was guessing (and n_keys=4 is the wrong difficulty to discriminate configs); if the model shows actual learning/climb, harder tasks force retrieval and may break the plateau.

config.name: timescale_mixture_exp011
config.hypothesis: Phase-2 mechanism variant: the .mean collapse over the 4 braided decay timescales (gdn3_upgrade.py:822) mashes distinct retention rates into one averaged factor, removing the selective long-range channel a key->value binding needs to survive 512 tokens. A learned per-timescale softmax mixture (tau_gate, zero-init so warm-start is unperturbed) lets the model route content to different retention rates and preserve a slow long-range channel — should break the ~0.30 format plateau toward true retrieval.

config.name: coprod_binding_init_exp012
config.hypothesis: Phase-2 mechanism variant: the coproduct key->value binding channel starts OFF at init (coprod_mix_qk/v = 0, dense-only) and must be turned on by gradients that barely flow while the dense path already satisfies the format shortcut. Initing coprod_mix_qk/v to 0.1 puts the bilinear binding partially ON from the start so it gets gradient — a coproduct-wiring init change, true to GDN3 math, forward-only. If retrieval emerges past ~0.30, binding strength was the gate; if not, the bottleneck is the read/router.

config.name: output_norm_bypass_exp013
config.hypothesis: Phase-2 mechanism variant: the per-head L2 output norm (F.normalize(routed)*sqrt(V)*self.norm) squashes the retrieval signal — it erases the magnitude difference between 'retrieved the right value' and 'guessed a format', leaving only direction, which CE on format tokens can satisfy alone. A small un-normalized residual bypass (+0.1*routed) lets the retrieval logit magnitude grow past the format ceiling while keeping the GDN3 norm for stability. If tokacc climbs past 0.30, normalization was capping retrieval; if not, the bottleneck is the lane router.

config.name: router_sharpen_exp014
config.hypothesis: Phase-2 mechanism variant: the lane router (softmax over M=4 lanes) is zero-init -> starts uniform (each lane weight 0.25), so the readout = mean of all 4 lanes' states, diluting any single lane's binding signal 4x at init; if router gradients are weak (format shortcut satisfied by frozen full-attention layers), routing stays near-uniform and retrieval is diluted. A learnable router temperature (init 1.0, parity-equiv) lets routing sharpen over training to select the binding-holding lane. If tokacc climbs past 0.30, routing dilution was the bottleneck; if not, the bottleneck is structural (not a GDN3 mechanism) -> human handoff.

config.name: baseline_800_fineeval_exp015
config.hypothesis: STRUCTURAL-CAUSE DOCUMENTATION (config-only, no source edit): exp001's exact stable config run to 800 steps with fine eval_every=25 yields a high-resolution plateau curve. If flat ~0.30 through 800 steps, the format plateau is a genuine stable ceiling (frozen full-attention format shortcut fully satisfied, GDN3 never learns retrieval) = decisive final evidence for the structural human handoff. If tokacc climbs past step 500, emergence was being cut off and the issue is step budget, reopening the search.

config.name: blend_asymmetric_exp016
config.hypothesis: Phase-2 mechanism variant (last fair-game item: two-timescale blend rule): the compaction blend applies the SAME slow_decay to both Kronecker factors; making the value-factor Bk blend FASTER (slow_decay^2 vs slow_decay) than the key-factor A gives recent value-writes a quicker path into long-term state while keeping key structure more stable — an asymmetry the single slow_decay config knob cannot express. Faster Bk retention should also be more stable (less state accumulation). If tokacc climbs past 0.30 the blend asymmetry was the bottleneck; if not, the 5-item Phase-2 fair-game list is fully exonerated.

config.name: sharp_read_exp017
config.hypothesis: Phase-3 departure (## DEPARTURES): the soft GDN3 read returns a mushy average over stored associations, so the frozen LM head can't distinguish 'retrieved' from 'guessed' and takes the digit-frequency format shortcut (~0.30). ADD a sharp content-addressable within-window read (softmax over prior writes j<i by decayed q.k similarity, reading the stored value) on top of the soft read — produces a near-one-hot retrieval signal when q matches a stored key, forcing the frozen LM head to use it. Keeps the soft GDN3 read + Kronecker state + two-timescale compaction intact. If tokacc climbs past 0.30 the soft read was the bottleneck; if not, confirms the bottleneck is the proxy/loss design.

config.name: rank64_nkeys8_dclamp997_exp018
config.hypothesis: exp010 (n_keys=8) diverged at the unstable clamp 0.999, so we never saw whether harder retrieval load can force emergence when stable. The priors flag a residual_rank x n_keys interaction: max exact buffer (rank 64) holds more bindings, and with n_keys=8 under real retrieval pressure at the STABLE clamp 0.997 (exp009: skip 0.036), the model has maximum capacity + harder task + stability. If tokacc climbs past 0.30 the capacity x difficulty interaction was the missing ingredient (falsifies the structural diagnosis, reopens the search); if flat the plateau is fundamental regardless of capacity.

config.name: seqlen128_exp019
config.hypothesis: all 18 prior runs used seq_len=512, where the queried binding must survive ~512 tokens of decay/distractors. A shorter seq_len=128 puts the queried binding much closer to where it's stated = the EASIEST retrieval regime. If retrieval emerges at 128, decay/distance was part of the bottleneck (falsifies the structural diagnosis, reopens the search); if the plateau holds even at the easiest retrieval distance, the frozen-full-attention format-shortcut diagnosis is strongly confirmed.

config.name: clip05_steps600_exp020
config.hypothesis: exp006's surprise (divergence is slow state-accumulation independent of LR) and the structural diagnosis (vanishing retrieval gradient) both point at the gradient regime. All 19 runs used clip=1.0; a TIGHTER clip (0.5) suppresses the unbounded gradient growth that causes late divergence AND preferentially damps large format-gradients, potentially letting smaller retrieval gradients survive — testing whether the gradient-clipping regime is part of the bottleneck. Uses steps=600 (room to see if tighter clipping lets the divergent late-trajectory stay stable and whether tokacc climbs). If tokacc rises or stability improves markedly, the gradient regime matters; if flat, confirms the structural ceiling.

config.name: output_gain_exp021
config.hypothesis: Phase-3 departure (## DEPARTURES): the structural diagnosis says the GDN3 retrieval signal is DROWNED by the frozen full-attention residual (frozen path produces the format, GDN3 gets vanishing retrieval gradient). Amplify the GDN3 output branch 3x relative to the frozen hidden_states residual so more of the output (and gradient) is forced through GDN3. Directly attacks the diagnosed root cause from the only editable side (can't edit the proxy). If tokacc climbs past 0.30 the signal-drowning was the issue and it's fixable from the GDN3 side; if flat/diverges, confirms the proxy itself must be edited.

config.name: lrmem8e4_seqlen128_exp022
config.hypothesis: every higher-LR run (4e-4+) diverged at seq_len=512 due to state accumulation over 512 tokens; at seq_len=128 the state accumulates 4x less, so the top-of-range lr_memory 8e-4 (never tried) might stay stable AND push hard enough on optimization to break the format plateau. Tests the LR x length interaction never directly probed: if tokacc climbs at 128+8e-4, length-mediated the LR divergence and high LR can retrieve; if it diverges even at 128, divergence is LR-driven not length-driven (confirms exp006); if stable-flat, plateau is fundamental regardless of optimization strength.

config.name: output_gain_10x_exp023
config.hypothesis: exp021 (3x GDN3 output gain) was stable but didn't break the plateau; the structural diagnosis predicts the GDN3 signal is drowned by the frozen residual accumulated across 18 layers, so a single layer's 3x may be far too small. 10x tests the magnitude threshold directly: if retrieval emerges, cumulative-residual-dominance was the issue and a large enough gain fixes it from the GDN3 side; if stable-flat, the magnitude hypothesis is definitively dead (3x AND 10x both fail -> airtight handoff); if diverges, the frozen out_proj can't handle large GDN3 signals (another proxy constraint).

config.name: lrmem1e4_steps900_exp024
config.hypothesis: FALSIFICATION TEST of the structural diagnosis (vanishing GDN3 retrieval gradient): 'vanishing' has a testable distinction — truly tiny/zero vs small-but-nonzero. exp006 (lr 1.5e-4, 800 steps) got 0.308 fragile; the MINIMUM-LR x MAXIMUM-TIME regime (lr 1e-4 floor, steps 900) is most likely to accumulate a small-but-nonzero retrieval signal. If tokacc climbs past 0.30 the gradient is merely small (diagnosis wrong, search reopens); if flat at ~0.30 through 900 steps at the floor LR, the gradient is genuinely vanishing (a small gradient would accumulate and climb) — strongly confirming the diagnosis.

config.name: zero_gdn3_diagnostic_exp025
config.hypothesis: DECISIVE DIAGNOSTIC (Phase-3 ## DEPARTURES): make every GDN3-upgraded layer a pure IDENTITY (return hidden_states unchanged) so the model = frozen full-attention layers + frozen LM head + residuals with GDN3 as no-ops. If tokacc stays ~0.30 with GDN3 IDENTITY, the frozen path fully produces the format and GDN3 is irrelevant to the proxy signal (diagnosis PROVEN airtight); if tokacc collapses to ~0 (random digits), GDN3 WAS contributing the format signal (diagnosis WRONG, search reopens). The one test that converts the inference into proof.

config.name: sharp_read_replace_exp026
config.hypothesis: CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS (exp025): the ~0.30 plateau is GDN3-LEARNED (a format shortcut), not frozen-path-produced. The soft within-window read (new_u * kq, a LINEAR key-query scaling) emits value even without a strong key match -> lets GDN3 satisfy CE with generic digits. REPLACE the soft value emission with a SHARP content-addressable read (softmax over prior writes by decayed q.k, reading the stored value) so value emission requires key match. exp017 ADDED this sharp term (soft read still satisfied format); this REPLACES the soft term. If tokacc climbs past 0.30 the soft read enabled the format shortcut and sharp-only forces retrieval; if flat/broken, the format shortcut isn't solely from the soft value term.

config.name: nocompact_rank64_seqlen64_exp027
config.hypothesis: LINEAR-READ vs COMPACTION ceiling separator. 26 prior configs ALL got recall 0.0 (incl exp017 which added a softmax read the model ignored). The GDN3 read is entirely linear-in-q (kron_q+old_q+prev_q+self all linear in q) so it can only emit a linear projection -> fits the marginal digit distribution (format, tokacc ~0.30) but structurally cannot do exact key->value retrieval (linear-capacity ceiling). But lossy SVD compaction (seq_len=512, P=16 = 32 windows) is a SECOND possible ceiling. Set residual_rank=64, seq_len=64 so P>=seq_len -> NO compaction, exact buffer for all tokens. If recall stays 0 -> linear read is definitively the ceiling (next: nonlinear/softmax read edit). If recall>0 -> compaction was the ceiling (next: attack state retention). Config-only, decisive separator.

config.name: softmax_read_exp028
config.hypothesis: THE FIX for the linear-read ceiling (exp027 PROVEN: 27 configs all recall 0.0; linear read can only emit a linear projection -> fits format marginal, cannot do exact key->value retrieval). REPLACE the within-window retrieval (old_q+prev_q, both LINEAR in q) with a SOFTMAX content-addressable read over the P most-recent writes (nonlinear in q -> CAN select the matching key's value). Math change, parity OK. Direct comparison to exp027 (same config P=64 seq_len=64 no compaction, linear read, recall 0.0). If recall>0 -> linear ceiling BROKEN (first nonzero recall in 28 experiments). Keeps kron_q (long-term, linear) + self-term (stability) intact; replaces the retrieval path itself (not add like exp017, not wrong term like exp026).

config.name: read_discrim_train_exp029
config.hypothesis: Does TRAINING make the GDN3 read discriminative, or keep it uniform? Diagnostic found read scores UNIFORM at init (entropy~ln(T)) -> uniform read = format marginal -> tokacc 0.30 recall 0. If entropy stays uniform after training + tokacc~0.30 + recall 0 -> CONFIRMS format-shortcut ceiling (no discrimination gradient; learnable-read-proj plan invalidated). If entropy drops -> coproduct CAN discriminate, bottleneck downstream.

config.name: sharp_softmax_scale01_exp030
config.hypothesis: AMPLIFY the weak-but-real read discrimination (exp029 PROVEN: value position rises to top 9% after training, but read stays ~95% uniform -> weak signal below the recall threshold). Replace the linear within-window retrieval with a SHARP SOFTMAX content-addressable read (scale=0.1, 113x sharper than exp028's sqrt(K)=11.3 which gave logits ~0.02 -> uniform -> recall 0). At scale=0.1, the ~0.3-0.5 value-score elevation becomes a DOMINANT softmax peak -> one-hot value selection -> recall. Direct comparison to exp028 (same config P=64 seq_len=64, scale=sqrt(K), tokacc 0.27 recall 0). Success=ANY recall>0 (first in 30 experiments). Risk: sharp read on near-uniform init scores picks noise -> may destabilize; watch tokacc curve.

config.name: sharp_read_1000steps_exp031
config.hypothesis: FORMAT ATTRACTOR structural test. exp030 (400 steps sharp) plateaued at 0.28; does 1000 steps break it? Trained sharp-softmax read 1000 steps + discrimination probe. RESULT: read BECAME SHARP (entropy 4.08->1.94, 47% of uniform) but sharp on WRONG positions (val_argmax 0.0, rank WORSE 0.09->0.17). The read mechanism works (can sharpen) but CE rewards format not retrieval -> no gradient to read the RIGHT value -> format attractor is STRUCTURAL to the CE-only loss. Confirms the loss, not the read, is the ceiling.

config.name: ideal_query_probe_exp032
config.hypothesis: WRITE/binding-side test. Is the correct value RETRIEVABLE? Probes: (1) self-retrieval (value own k -> value pos), (2) key->value binding (key k -> value pos), (3) actual q. CORRECTED INTERPRETATION: self works (peak 0.43, rank 0.01) -> keys distinguishable. binding FAILS (peak 0.0, rank 0.64) but CONFOUNDED: no-compaction regime (P=64,seq_len=64) has Kronecker state A,Bk=0 -> GDN3 coproduct binding is OFF, only linear residual buffer operates. So binding test is not testing the GDN3 binding mechanism. v_cosine_diff 0.97 is avg-then-cosine (misleading); per-head cosine-then-avg=0.58, best head 0.26 -> values ARE distinguishable. LM head digit rows cosine 0.43-0.78 (NOT 1.0; the 1.0 was a tokenization artifact) -> LM head CAN output digits. Two putative ceilings (v-collapsed, LM-head-identical) were artifacts. Remaining untested: does the Kronecker coproduct binding form WITH compaction (seq_len=512)?

corrected_note: v_cosine_diff 0.97 = avg-then-cosine (misleading); per-head = 0.58 avg / 0.26 best. LM head digit rows cosine 0.43-0.78 (distinguishable). Binding test confounded by no-compaction (Kronecker off).

config.name: kron_binding_compact_exp033
config.hypothesis: Does the Kronecker coproduct binding form WITH compaction (seq_len=512, P=16)? exp032 was confounded (no-compaction, A,Bk=0). Rebuild state at answer pos, probe with actual q vs key's k vs random. If ideal>>random -> binding forms (then query mismatch=loss ceiling). If ideal~random -> binding doesn't form -> editable WRITE-side lever.

config.name: tie_qk_coprod_exp034
config.hypothesis: QUERY-KEY ALIGNMENT via structural tie. exp033 PROVEN: the Kronecker binding FORMS with compaction (ideal key-query retrieves value, cos 0.35) but the ACTUAL answer-position query mismatches the stored key (cos 0.10). The coproduct q/k use SEPARATE projections (W_q_a vs W_k_a); tying them (k_a=q_a, k_b=q_b by computation) removes a degree of freedom so the same input produces matching q and k. The model still has the dense (frozen) q/k untied, but the coproduct (trainable) contribution to q and k is now identical. Hypothesis: this structural symmetry lets CE find query-key alignment faster -> recall emerges. If recall>0 -> alignment is the lever and the tie fixes it. If recall=0 -> the tie alone insufficient (CE still doesn't reward alignment; loss ceiling stands). Direct comparison to exp001 (seq_len=512, untied, tokacc 0.296 recall 0).

config.name: coprod_mix_high_exp035
config.hypothesis: Is the FROZEN DENSE q the format shortcut? exp033 found actual query (cos 0.10) mismatches stored key (cos 0.35). The query is 50% frozen-dense + 50% trainable-coproduct (sigmoid(0)=0.5). Force coproduct-dominated (coprod_mix_qk init 5.0, sigmoid=0.993 -> 99.3% coproduct, 0.7% dense) so the TRAINABLE coproduct fully controls q/k. If recall>0 -> the frozen dense q was the format shortcut, the coproduct CAN align -> EDITABLE BREAKTHROUGH. If 0.28/0.0 -> CE drives even coproduct-only query to format -> CE-only loss is the ceiling regardless of query source. Value mix stays at 0 (dense value encoding intact, exp033 showed value stored correctly). exp012 only nudged mix to 0.1; this is the extreme case (5.0).

config.name: dense_induction_test_exp036
config.hypothesis: Can the FROZEN dense q/k geometry do MQAR induction (match same-word-diff-context)? If yes->editable side can exploit it; if no->frozen-geometry ceiling, MUST unfreeze in_proj_qkv (human).

config.name: aggproj_headselect_exp037
config.hypothesis: Is head SELECTION the lever? exp036 found ~5/16 frozen-dense heads induce but _agg_proj (TRAINABLE, sees all 16 heads) dilutes them. _agg_proj already has capacity to up-weight inducing heads, so a per-head GATE would be redundant. This tests H2 (signal too weak to drive _agg_proj) vs H1 (CE never exploits it): zero _agg_proj's weights for half the heads at init (force the aggregation through 8 heads only). If recall>0 -> head selection matters, the restricted path lets CE find induction (editable). If 0.29/0.0 -> CE doesn't exploit even a head-restricted path -> loss-side ceiling, handoff airtight. Init-time mask (recurrence math unchanged -> parity OK). Direct comparison to exp001 (all 16 heads, 0.296/0.0).

config.name: marginal_tokacc_exp038
config.hypothesis: Is the tokacc 0.30 plateau PURE FORMAT (marginal digits) or PARTIAL RETRIEVAL? Compute per-position marginal digit tokacc. If marginal~0.30 -> pure format, handoff final. If marginal~0.10 and model~0.30 -> partial retrieval exists, reopens editable side.

config.name: aggproj_targeted_exp039
config.hypothesis: TARGETED head selection. exp039-step1 ranked 16 heads by consistent induction gap: top-5=[7,10,12,3,9] (mean gap 0.02-0.029, beat rest in 73% of episodes), bottom-11 are noise/anti-inducing. exp037 zeroed a RANDOM half -> 0.30/0.0. This zeros _agg_proj for the NON-inducing heads (keep top-5 only) to concentrate the partial-retrieval signal (exp038: 0.30 = 3.5x format, ~14%/digit). If concentrating the inducing heads pushes per-digit 14% higher and recall emerges -> editable side reopens. If still 0.30/0.0 -> signal too weak/noisy to amplify structurally -> handoff final. Init-time mask (parity OK). Direct comparison to exp001 (all 16 heads, 0.296/0.0) and exp037 (random 8 heads, 0.30/0.0).

config.name: lm_head_alignment_exp040
config.hypothesis: Is the frozen LM head ALIGNED to the GDN3 output subspace for digits? If the retrieval signal lives orthogonal to the digit rows, even perfect retrieval can't produce digits -> NEW ceiling (LM-head alignment). If aligned -> loss is the only barrier (handoff final).

config.name: nkeys2_exp041
config.hypothesis: DIFFICULTY test: is the partial-retrieval ceiling task-difficulty-bound? Every prior config used n_keys=4 or 8 (recall 0). At n_keys=2, only 2 distractors, so the ~14%/digit partial-retrieval signal (exp038) should be much stronger — possibly enough for exact recall. If recall>0 emerges -> the ceiling is difficulty-relative (easier tasks break it; the search reopens with difficulty-aware configs). If still 0.30/0.0 -> the CE-only loss is DEFINITIVELY the ceiling regardless of difficulty (handoff final). Direct comparison to exp001 (n_keys=4, 0.296/0.0). Config-only, ~18 min.
